_handoff: Multi-user epic backlog items — authored cycle 135 (2026-07-16) in the isolated planning worktree agent-a440cccdeb1075600. This worktree is based on the OLD #140 state, so its backlog.json is STALE (updated 2026-07-12) and must NOT be committed/merged as-is (it would clobber cycles 125-134). LANDING INSTRUCTIONS: after PR #141 (v1.1.8) merges and integration/next is recut, insert the objects in `items` below into the CURRENT backlog.json items array via TARGETED string edits (never json.load/dump — see lesson backlog-json-duplicate-keys), then diff-check dup ids with the python Counter check. Also carry specs/multi-user-epic-plan.md over. Note the PM pass provisionally added ids `multiuser-profiles-discovery-connect` and `virtual-match-mvp` to THIS stale copy only — do NOT double-file; use the canonical ids below (multiuser-p1-*/multiuser-p2-*). Suggested backlog note-header append: 'Cycle 135 (2026-07-16): OWNER MULTI-USER EPIC greenlit + planned. Fable architecture spec at specs/multi-user-epic-plan.md (P0 secure multi-account foundation / P1 profiles+discovery+connect / P2 Virtual Match). Filed multiuser-epic tracker + 4 P0 slices (ready) + P1/P2 (blocked-on-P0). Every phase /security-review-gated; single-user byte-identical until each phase flips APP_ACCESS_MODE.'

items.title: EPIC: Multi-user Looper — secure accounts, profiles/connect, Virtual Match (App Store)
items.why: Owner greenlit 2026-07-16 (verbatim): 'make this multi user... distributed on the App Store... a very secure login system, and ability to connect with users and search their profiles connect via phone number, email... Then I want to implement that virtual match capability.' Phased, security-first program in specs/multi-user-epic-plan.md (Plan agent on fable + grounded auth audit + PM P1/P2 shapes + reviewer P0 threat-model). KEY FINDINGS: backend is ALREADY multi-tenant-shaped (row scoping by verified Clerk sub is pervasive; require_owner is a single env-var gate at main.py:62 over 17 routers) so 'the flip' is small; the real work is closing 3 unscoped-route gaps (pins/job-ids/personas), an authz-isolation test suite, and the CLIENT (derive 'me' from Clerk, per-user localStorage namespacing, Keychain token). RLS DECISION: app-layer scoping for P0 + a user_session() centralization that makes RLS a drop-in later hardening (deferred to end of P1). This is the parent tracker; buildable work = the multiuser-p0-* slices below. See the OWNER-DECISION list in the spec (Clerk plan/pricing, phone-SMS costs, discoverability default opt-in, migration timing, App Store account-deletion, hole_pins per-user, RLS-now-or-later, LLM cost per stranger, launch ramp).

items.title: P0: per-user authz flip (require_member) + close unscoped-route gaps + isolation test suite
items.why: specs/multi-user-epic-plan.md §3.1/§3.3/§3.6 — the FIRST buildable P0 slice. (1) Add APP_ACCESS_MODE env (owner|open) in clerk_auth.py + a require_member dep: owner-mode = byte-identical to today's require_owner; open-mode = any verified Clerk sub. Swap main.py:62 _owner_only -> _member on all 17 routers; keep require_owner on the by-nature owner-only endpoints (caller-voice/rehearsal-call, tee_times.py:633/767/787). (2) Close the gaps the flip exposes: [BLOCKING, reviewer pass] carve courses_mapped.py POST/PUT/DELETE (:68/:109/:126 — member-writable GLOBAL course geometry, no owner column; PUT/DELETE re-precompute elevations+guides so a stranger corrupts every user's caddie yardages) to STAY require_owner post-flip + negative write-authz test; hole_pins -> per-user (add user_id, unique key (course,hole,date,user_id), scope pins.py list/upsert — recommended, protects caddie yardage integrity; owner decision #6); stamp+match user_id on the _availability_jobs dict + any future async course-search job (uuid is not authz; NOTE scorecard /scan is synchronous, no job-id — and request_availability_call must stay owner-only OR require the member's own callbackNumber, never fall back to the owner's number); caddie_personas read=is_builtin|is_public|author==me, write/delete author-scoped 404. (3) Introduce a canonical user_session(user_id) acquisition helper every tenant route migrates to (no behavior change now; the single seam RLS plugs into later) + a CI scoping-lint. (4) The AUTHZ-ISOLATION TEST SUITE is the acceptance bar: parametrized per-router A-creates/B-gets-404, LIST excludes, mutate-404-and-unchanged; 404-not-403 enumeration property identical to random-nonexistent-id; flip-regression (owner-mode 403s non-owner, open-mode both pass). Harness already supports identity injection (tests/integration/conftest.py:176-184). MAJOR/auth -> /security-review + /code-review MANDATORY before ready. Single-user stays byte-identical (owner mode default). No migration RUN in this slice (design only).

items.title: P0: owner-row backfill migration (design) + Clerk webhook revocation + azp/boot-guard hardening
items.why: specs/multi-user-epic-plan.md §3.2/§3.4 — the security+data slice. (1) Backfill/tighten migration DESIGN (guarded-migration process, owner-approval-gated to RUN): verify-first read-only query (count NULL owner_id / distinct owner_id) on rounds/tournaments/players/scoring_courses/caddie_sessions/shots — prod likely already stamps the owner's real Clerk id so backfill may be a no-op; Migration A backfills owner_id IN (NULL,'anonymous')->OWNER_CLERK_USER_ID (reviewer: 'anonymous'-stamped rows from ALLOW_ANONYMOUS/no-JWKS eras are neither NULL nor owner and would orphan if only NULL is handled; assert no owner_id outside {NULL,'anonymous',OWNER} pre-run); Migration B (after >=1wk soak) SET NOT NULL. (2) Clerk webhook receiver POST /api/webhooks/clerk (Svix-signature-verified, NOT member-gated) handling user.deleted/user.banned -> revoked_users table checked in require_member (60s cache); this is also the App Store account-deletion anchor (owner decision #5, cascade-delete per the table list, but preserve historical shared scorecards). (3) azp/authorized-parties verification (CLERK_AUTHORIZED_PARTIES) to close cross-app token replay (aud currently unverified) — reviewer: reject a token whose azp is ABSENT or not in the allowlist (not just present-mismatched), and in open-mode boot-require both CLERK_ISSUER and CLERK_AUTHORIZED_PARTIES (issuer is signature-only today when CLERK_ISSUER unset). (4) Fail-open boot guards: refuse to boot if open-mode + (no JWKS or ALLOW_ANONYMOUS); loud-warn owner-mode + unset OWNER_CLERK_USER_ID; CI assertion NEXT_PUBLIC_AUTH_BYPASS unset in prod builds. MAJOR/auth+migration+new-endpoint -> /security-review MANDATORY. Migration files are guarded — do NOT write without owner approval; this slice DESIGNS + writes the webhook/azp/guards code only.

items.title: P0: client — derive 'me' from Clerk + per-user localStorage namespacing + offline-leak fix
items.why: specs/multi-user-epic-plan.md §3.5 — the client foundation (the real multi-user work). (1) lib/identity.ts + useMe() (wraps useUser(); ensure the golfer_profiles row + a self SavedPlayer with clerkUserId=me exist on sign-in). (2) 'this is me' default: round setup defaults ownerIndex to the roster player whose clerkUserId===me (pill stays as a rare override; no visual change). (3) Per-user localStorage namespacing: key(name)=scorecard_${userId}_${name} across the 5 data keys (storage.ts:5-9) AND pref stores (caddie persona, favorites, tts, live-mode, map-view); one-time migrate legacy un-namespaced keys into the current user's namespace (device data is definitionally the owner's today) + scorecard_migrated_v1 flag; settings 'clear data' clears only the current namespace (today's localStorage.clear() nukes other users). (4) Offline-fallback identity leak: storage-api.ts picks API-vs-local via !!window.Clerk?.session (identity-blind) — persist scorecard_last_user_id, serve ONLY that user's namespace offline, never another user's cache. Byte-identical for the owner (single account). User-facing capability -> designer NORTHSTAR review; gates lint+tsc+build+voice-smoke + new storage unit tests (namespacing, user-switch sees empty, offline refuses foreign cache).

items.title: P0: native JWT -> iOS Keychain (drop plaintext Capacitor Preferences token)
items.why: specs/multi-user-epic-plan.md §3.4 — App-Store-grade token-at-rest. lib/native-token-store.ts persists the Clerk session JWT PLAINTEXT in Capacitor Preferences (flagged in-file :12-15). Swap the backend for a Keychain-backed secure-storage plugin (kSecAttrAccessibleWhenUnlockedThisDeviceOnly — encrypted at rest, excluded from iCloud/device backups); the file was written for a one-file swap. Include a one-time migrate-then-delete of the plaintext Preferences value. New iOS dependency (justify per CLAUDE.md) — verify the Capacitor webview Clerk login (native onBeforeRequest/onAfterResponse hooks) still round-trips the token on device. Security hardening; independent of the flip (can land anytime). /security-review (token handling).

items.title: P1: profiles + privacy-first discovery (hashed phone/email) + connect/blocks
items.why: specs/multi-user-epic-plan.md §4 (+ PM product shape). BLOCKED until the P0 isolation foundation ships. Extend golfer_profiles (handle, avatar_url, discoverable_by_email/phone default OFF, public_fields, profileVisibility). user_identifiers lookup index = HMAC-SHA256(normalized identifier, server pepper in Secrets Manager) — raw identifiers NEVER stored/queryable; rows written ONLY from Clerk webhooks (verified identifiers). user_connections (request/accept, mutual, unordered-pair unique) + user_blocks (silent, total, severs). Formalize name-string rosters via players.clerk_user_id ('Link to @mike' not a duplicate row). API: POST /users/lookup (exact handle/email/phone, no list/prefix, per-user + per-IP quota, blocked==nonexistent), connections/blocks CRUD, GET /users/{id} connected-only-or-404. Partners hub evolves /players in place (NO new bottom-tab — real nav is Home/Courses/Tee-times/Profile). App Store: native per-contact picker (no blanket Contacts perm), privacy-label update, account-deletion cascade covers identifiers/connections/blocks. RECOMMENDED point to land RLS (owner decision #7). /security-review MANDATORY (identifier-hash design, webhook sig, enumeration/scraping resistance). Cycle count ~3. Owner decisions #1/#2/#3/#8/#12/#13.

items.title: P2: Virtual Match MVP — async net match-play round-vs-round with live standings
items.why: specs/multi-user-epic-plan.md §5 (+ PM MVP shape) — the owner's headline ask. BLOCKED on P1 (needs the connection graph). MVP = two CONNECTED users each play their OWN round (existing scoring untouched) on a locked course/tee; a virtual_matches overlay computes NET match-play with a live/near-live standings feed. GROUNDING: computeMatchPlay (games.ts:739) is GROSS-only today — net allocation via HoleInfo.handicap is GENUINELY NEW logic (new pure lib/virtual-match.ts + a Python backend twin with parity tests vs games.ts fixtures, since the client can't read the opponent's raw round). Schema: virtual_matches (creator/opponent, status, per-side round_ids, locked course/tee, frozen_handicaps snapshot, result, expires_at) + optional Round.virtualMatchId mirroring tournamentId. State machine proposed->accepted->active->completed (+declined/cancelled/expired); consent structural (nothing visible pre-accept; attach-only-own-round); expiry void unless zero-hole no-show (owner decision #11). ANTI-TAMPER: neither can touch the other's scores (falls out of P0); opponent sees a minimized scores-only projection (never GPS/caddie/memories). Client: 'Challenge' on a Partner profile + quiet match card + result screen (reuse leaderboard/SettleUp visual language, NOT a new tab); caddie answers 'how's my match with Mike' as DATA not prompt-trusted text. DEFERRED: simultaneous/live, groups/leagues, money settlement, chat, rematches. /security-review + designer NORTHSTAR review. Cycle count ~3-4.
